name: Trusting HTTP Permission Methods on the Server Side
description: The server-side protection mechanism assumes that HTTP GET requests will not cause state changes, so it only applies access controls to POST/PUT/DELETE methods. Attackers can bypass these controls by using GET requests to perform state-changing operations that should be restricted.
detection_pattern: AUTH or CONTROL node applies access restrictions based on HTTP method, permitting GET requests to reach STORAGE or TRANSFORM nodes that perform state-changing operations. Look for security filters that only check POST/PUT/DELETE while allowing GET to pass through unchecked to mutation endpoints.
